pattern: Sort words by length and let each word extend the best chain among the words you get by deleting one of its characters, a dynamic program keyed on the strings themselves.
hints: A predecessor is always exactly one character shorter, so process words from shortest to longest.
For a given word, every possible predecessor is obtained by deleting one of its letters — there are only as many candidates as the word is long.
Keep best[word] = longest chain ending at word; for each word try all single-deletion variants and take the best existing chain plus one.

edge_inputs.description: chain of length four embedded among extras
letters reordered, no valid chain past length one

stress.description: random short words built mostly by single insertions to create chains
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    words = set()
    seeds = [''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 3))) for _ in range(5)]
    for w in seeds:
        words.add(w)
    pool = list(seeds)
    while len(words) < n and pool:
        base = rng.choice(pool)
        if len(base) >= 16:
            continue
        pos = rng.randint(0, len(base))
        ch = rng.choice('ab')
        nw = base[:pos] + ch + base[pos:]
        words.add(nw)
        pool.append(nw)
        if rng.random() < 0.3:
            words.add(''.join(rng.choice('ab') for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 5))))
    return [list(words)]
stress.note: single-insertion growth seeds real chains; tiny alphabet for density
